mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in FEDFUNDS transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is confirmed when rate cuts are followed by positive forward equity returns; it is refuted when rate declines occur without a corresponding improvement in SPX returns.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of months after a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's 3-year rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a short-run threshold signals weakening household income and demand, which pressures corporate revenues and lowers S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should appear most clearly after a measurable deterioration in labor conditions, not during stable unemployment.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered events do not produce negative 20d SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if returns are positive on average after unemployment shocks despite flat or falling PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad demand acceleration in consumption and housing, which improves earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails when demand gains are confined to only one series without broad confirmation.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS/HOUST up-moves do not lead to positive 60d SPX returns in at least 60% of instances, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power versus either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly improvement in INDPRO after prior weakness signals stronger real activity and inventory repair, which should boost cyclical earnings expectations and deliver positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should be strongest when industrial output is rising faster than its recent trend.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by at least 0.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO acceleration does not precede positive 20d SPX returns in at least 55% of triggered observations, or if the sign reverses in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is decelerating while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real policy tightness eases and equity discount rates stop rising, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism is specific to disinflation under restrictive policy, not to low inflation alone.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change falls below 2.5% while FEDFUNDS stays above 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation-plus-restrictive-policy regimes do not outperform baseline by at least 2 percentage points over 60d, or if the combined proxy signal is not significant after controlling for each series individually.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in PAYEMS indicates payroll creation and income support, which broadens spending power and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal should work when employment growth is broad-based and fail when payroll gains are offset by rising unemployment.
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS momentum does not correspond to positive 20d SPX returns more often than not, or if the average forward return after payroll gains is not above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 relative to its prior quarter signals demand contraction and weaker earnings growth expectations, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect is strongest when the GDP slowdown aligns with falling retail sales and housing activity.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines on a quarter-over-quarter basis
failure_condition: Reject if GDP contraction signals do not precede negative 60d SPX returns in at least 55% of triggered quarters, or if adding RSAFS and HOUST does not improve prediction over GDPC1 alone.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in INDPRO and PAYEMS after prior weakness signals broad real-sector stabilization, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through faster earnings revisions and lower recession odds. The mechanism fails when industrial output improves without payroll confirmation.
failure_condition: Reject if joint INDPRO/PAYEMS improvements do not generate positive 5d SPX returns in at least 58% of cases, or if the interaction signal loses significance once either proxy is removed.
